title: Write a Prometheus Alerting Rule
description:
## Alert on High Error Rate

Prometheus rule files contain **`groups`**, each with a list of **`rules`**. An alerting rule has an **`alert`** name, an **`expr`**, and a **`for`** duration.

**Your task:** write **`/root/rules.yml`** with a group containing an alerting rule named **`HighErrorRate`** that has an `expr` and a `for`.

> 💡 Validate the YAML with `python3 -c "import yaml; yaml.safe_load(open('/root/rules.yml'))"`.

hints.title: Rule group skeleton
hints.body: A groups list; each group has a name and rules; an alerting rule sets alert/expr/for plus optional labels and annotations.
hints.command:
printf 'groups:
  - name: app-alerts
    rules:
      - alert: HighErrorRate
        expr: rate(http_errors_total[5m]) > 0.05
        for: 10m
        labels:
          severity: page
        annotations:
          summary: "High error rate"
' > /root/rules.yml

validation.description: Checks a valid groups/rules structure and the HighErrorRate alert with expr+for.

commands.command: python3 -c "import yaml; d=yaml.safe_load(open('/root/rules.yml')); g=d.get('groups'); print('ok' if isinstance(g,list) and g and 'rules' in g[0] else 'no')"

commands.description: Defines a HighErrorRate alert with an expr and a for
